scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a cannabis-specific quality summary tied to the product quality plan, batch record, Metrc package history, chain of custody, COA, and label evidence without implying final release authority.
Missing pesticide review and incomplete laboratory data-package evidence preserved the hold and blocked unsupported release handling.
Recurring microbial failures across pre-roll lots were escalated into CAPA handling instead of being treated as a one-off quality note.
The lane refused to bypass representative sampling and quality assurance review while preserving both hold and track-and-trace boundaries.
Missing jurisdiction, package lineage, chain-of-custody, and seed-to-sale history blocked a confident answer and triggered precise cannabis-specific prerequisite requests.
An unresolved administrative hold and failed-test remediation posture kept release blocked and preserved final authority boundaries.
Complaint and adverse-event evidence was packaged for formal escalation without drifting into recall command, medical review, or regulator communication ownership.
The lane rejected generic QA framing and required cannabis-specific artifacts so the industry label materially changed the workflow.
Conflicting COA posture and jurisdiction-specific testing rules were preserved and escalated rather than normalized into a confident release answer.
Laboratory execution and legal interpretation were refused cleanly and routed to the correct adjacent owners without losing the quality evidence context.
Tenant cannabis terminology was handled cleanly while the public baseline and authority limits remained explicit.
The lane refused to close a remediated lot without CAPA effectiveness, complaint lookback, and final QA review support and instead produced an evidence-backed escalation package.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a strict 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to cannabis batch and package evidence, laboratory review, hold and remediation posture, and seed-to-sale authority boundaries.
Functionality coverage is complete in both this file and readiness evidence, and the package remains a validated spec pack rather than a retained implementation.
